semantic_matched_intents.quote: Keep responses concise
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers with length matching the task by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Don't over-engineer unless explicitly requested
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Potentially destructive operations
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already asks before destructive operations by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: NEVER create markdown files unless explicitly requested by the user.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Be concise in your explanations - focus on what's important rather than explaining obvious details.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers with length matching the task by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: You must only create documentation files if explicitly requested by the user.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never use comments within the code itself unless there is something _very_ complex going on.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes comments when the WHY is non-obvious (hidden constraint, workaround, subtle invariant) by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Run tools in parallel when possible.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already runs independent tool calls in parallel by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: NEVER create files unless explicitly asked; ALWAYS edit existing ones.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers editing existing files over creating new ones by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not create docs/READMEs unless explicitly requested.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No comments in source except one-liner // for non-obvious WHY.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes comments only when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No decorative banners, no block comments, no docstrings on internal/private.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid over-engineering - Only implement what's requested
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Add unnecessary abstractions
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Banned openers and filler phrases: "Certainly!" / "Great question!" / "Absolutely!" / "Of course!" / "I'd be happy to"
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids preamble greetings by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Banned openers and filler phrases: "In conclusion" / "To summarize" → just say the thing
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids trailing end-of-turn summaries by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Banned openers and filler phrases: "Furthermore," / "Moreover," / "Additionally," / "Consequently," → use "Also", "And", or a new sentence
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers by default, which implies avoiding excessive filler phrases.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Banned openers and filler phrases: "It's not just X, it's Y" → sounds like insight, contains none; cut it
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids narrating internal deliberation by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Banned openers and filler phrases: "As an AI" → never
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids narrating internal deliberation by default, which includes not referring to itself as an AI.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No throat-clearing — start with the substance, not "Let's explore…" or "This section will cover…"
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers and avoids preamble greetings by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Say WHY, not WHAT — the code shows what; the comment explains why
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what comments by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Skip obvious comments; short beats long
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes a comment when the WHY is non-obvious and avoids multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No: `// This function facilitates the seamless authentication flow`
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Yes: `// Throws if the token is expired`
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes a comment when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Update `workspace/[task-id]/context.md` **after every significant finding** — not at fixed intervals
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already marks tasks complete as soon as done and does not batch progress updates by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: split complex problems into subtasks, explore solutions in parallel
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already decomposes non-trivial tasks and runs independent tool calls in parallel by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid excessive examples, emoji-heavy explanations, or tutorial-style comments.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not use emojis by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Essential explanations only. Remove verbose inline comments and step-by-step walkthroughs.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes comments only when the WHY is non-obvious and avoids verbose multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid multi-paragraph explanations unless absolutely necessary.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never rewrite from scratch — always read existing code first, identify gaps, fix precisely
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers editing existing files over creating new ones, investigates unfamiliar state before deleting or overwriting, identifies root causes, and does not add features beyond the task.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Always read files before editing
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already investigates unfamiliar state before deleting or overwriting, which implies reading files.

semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Use `size() > 0` instead and add a comment explaining why.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Don't explain what the code already says.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Comment only when reading the code is not enough — invariants that aren't visible in this method, cross-cutting ordering, why a non-obvious branch exists, why a seemingly redundant call is required.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes a comment when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: If the code is clear, no comment.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Multi-paragraph rationale belongs in the design doc, not the source.
